# This is good. Now add one more perspective in. That perspective must be a full stack full wire production live build agentic coding team hoarde. Review each role and each function and action of each role. Then apply it to this build. The. Rewrite the full project inclusive of all updates and upgrades and instead of human timeframes use the agent hoardes perspective. Please proceed

Good. Now let me deliver the complete LexCore v3.0 — the full definitive spec.

***

# LexCore v3.0 — Complete Production Specification

## *Optimized Through 8 Team Perspectives + Agentic Build Horde*

**This is the only document you need.** Every lens applied, every gap closed, every human timeline eliminated. When you approve, the Horde executes.

***

## The Horde's Verdict on v2.0

The Agentic Coding Horde reviewed the previous plan and flagged the following before reconstruction: no shared project manifest (agents would drift), no branch-per-lane discipline, no self-verification step per agent, no dependency graph between agents, and no contract between lanes (agents in L3 were referencing schema objects that L1 hadn't finalized yet). All five issues are corrected in v3.0.[^1][^2]

***

## THE AGENTIC CODING HORDE

The Horde is a **24-agent, 7-lane, Sync-Lane Protocol build system**. Agents execute in parallel within each lane. Lanes execute in serial order. No lane starts until the previous lane's CriticAgent gate passes. Real-world benchmarks show 4-agent hordes completing in minutes with 98.4% build success rates — this is the execution model, not human sprints.[^1][^3][^2]

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### The Sync-Lane Protocol Rules

1. **Every agent reads `AGENTS.md` before starting.** This is the living project manifest maintained by `ContextKeeper`. It contains: current schema version, all approved contracts, all completed outputs, all open blockers.
2. **No agent writes code that references another lane's output without a formal contract.** Contracts are defined in `AGENTS.md` before any lane begins.
3. **Every agent self-verifies** before submitting to `CriticAgent`. Self-verification = run your own test, lint your own output, check your output against the contract.
4. **`CriticAgent` gates every lane.** Any output scoring below the quality gate is returned to the originating agent with specific revision instructions — not escalated to a human.
5. **Branch-per-lane discipline.** Each lane works on `lane/N-name`. Merges to `main` only after `CriticAgent` gate passes and `HordeMaster` promotes.

***

### Lane 0 — Command \& Control

**`HordeMaster`** — Reads full LexCore v3.0 spec. Decomposes into lane contracts. Assigns agents. Monitors gate states. Resolves blockers between agents. Promotes lanes to `main`. The only agent that communicates across all lanes. Never writes application code.

*Systematic functions:* spec decomposition → dependency graph generation → lane contract authoring → agent assignment → gate monitoring → blocker resolution → promotion decisions → final integration orchestration

**`ContextKeeper`** — Maintains `AGENTS.md` as the single source of truth for all agents. Updates it after every agent output is approved. All agents read it before every task. Prevents context drift across the horde.

*Systematic functions:* manifest initialization → schema version tracking → contract registry → completed output registry → blocker log → agent state tracking

**`CriticAgent`** — Reviews every agent output against the gate criteria for that lane. Returns structured revision requests on failure. Approves and records on pass. Never writes code, never makes product decisions.

*Systematic functions:* output receipt → contract compliance check → code quality review → test coverage verification → schema alignment check → approval/revision dispatch → gate state update in `AGENTS.md`

***

### Lane 1 — Foundation *(runs after AEU-0)*

Four agents run in **full parallel**. Each has a formal contract defined in `AGENTS.md` before starting. No agent waits for another in this lane.

**`SchemaArchitect`** — Writes the complete Alembic migration set. Generates seed data for all jurisdictions. Writes migration tests. Validates referential integrity, RLS policies, and all indexes before submitting.

*Systematic functions:* migration file generation → table creation ordering → index creation → RLS policy scripts → seed data → `alembic upgrade head` self-test → schema contract publication to `AGENTS.md`

**`DockerCompose`** — Writes `docker-compose.yml` (dev), `docker-compose.prod.yml`, and all `Dockerfile`s. Defines service startup order (Postgres → Redis → Ollama → Celery → API). Writes health check scripts per service.

*Systematic functions:* service dependency mapping → volume definitions → network definitions → env variable injection → health check scripting → startup order enforcement → self-test with `docker compose up --dry-run`

**`EnvConfig`** — Writes `.env.example` with every required variable and documentation. Writes `config.py` (Pydantic `BaseSettings`). Writes `validate_env.sh` that fails loudly on missing required vars before any service starts.

*Systematic functions:* variable inventory → required vs optional classification → Pydantic settings model → validation script → secret reference mapping (no hardcoded values) → self-test against docker-compose env expectations

**`CICDBuilder`** — Writes complete GitHub Actions workflows: `ci.yml` (lint + test on every PR), `deploy-canary.yml` (10% traffic canary, 48h hold), `deploy-promote.yml` (full promote after gate), `rollback.yml` (one-command rollback to previous image).

*Systematic functions:* workflow YAML authoring → test gate definition → canary step scripting → promotion criteria definition → rollback trigger scripting → secret injection from GitHub Secrets → self-test with `act` (local GitHub Actions runner)

**Lane 1 Gate Criteria:** All migrations run clean on fresh Postgres. Docker Compose starts all services. All env vars documented. CI workflow triggers correctly on test PR.

***

### Lane 2 — Backend Core *(runs after Lane 1 gate passes)*

Four agents in **full parallel**, all referencing the finalized schema contract from Lane 1.

**`APIBuilder`** — Writes all FastAPI 0.115 routes, Pydantic v2 request/response models, dependency injection, background tasks, and OpenAPI documentation. Writes route-level integration tests.

*Systematic functions:* route definition → Pydantic model authoring → dependency injection setup → background task registration → error handler registration → OpenAPI tag grouping → route-level tests → self-test with `httpx.AsyncClient`

**`MCPServerAgent`** — Implements all 7 MCP tools with full MCP SDK compliance: `get_capabilities`, `search_legal`, `research_task`, `get_document`, `get_citations`, `check_updates`, `jurisdiction_summary`. Writes tool schema validation tests.

*Systematic functions:* tool schema authoring → tool handler implementation → capability manifest builder → MCP protocol compliance check → tool input validation → streaming response setup → self-test with MCP inspector

**`AuthGuard`** — Implements API key hashing + validation, JWT (access + refresh tokens), rate limiter (per-tenant, per-tier limits), RLS context injection (`SET app.tenant_id`), and audit log writes on every authenticated request.

*Systematic functions:* API key hash/verify functions → JWT encode/decode → rate limiter middleware → RLS context setter → auth dependency injection → tier-based limit enforcement → audit log writer → self-test: attempt unauthorized request, verify 401

**`CacheLayer`** — Implements Redis client, query fingerprinting (SHA256 of normalized payload), cache set/get/invalidate, TTL management per query type, cache warming strategy, and cache hit metrics.

*Systematic functions:* Redis client setup → fingerprint function → cache middleware → TTL configuration table → invalidation triggers (on document update) → hit/miss metrics → self-test: identical queries return identical results

**Lane 2 Gate Criteria:** All routes return correct status codes on test payloads. MCP tools pass schema validation. Unauthenticated requests are rejected. Cache hit/miss correctly logged.

***

### Lane 3 — Data Pipeline *(runs after Lane 2 gate passes)*

**`IngestArchitect`** runs first and publishes the Celery task contract. Then **all SourceAdapters + ChunkingEngineer + EmbeddingWorker run in parallel**.[^1]

**`IngestArchitect`** — Designs the complete Celery task queue architecture. Defines task signatures, retry policies, dead-letter queue routing, worker concurrency settings, beat schedule (per-source cron), and the `ingest_jobs` table write contract.

*Systematic functions:* task signature definition → retry policy (3 attempts, exponential backoff) → dead-letter routing → beat schedule authoring → worker pool configuration → Flower monitoring setup → task contract publication to `AGENTS.md`

**`SourceAdapter[CanLII]`** — Scrapes CanLII via their API (respectful rate limiting). Handles bilingual (EN/FR) documents. Extracts: title, citation, body_of_law, jurisdiction, effective_date, full text. Writes to `ingest_jobs` log on each batch.

**`SourceAdapter[eCFR]`** — Consumes eCFR XML API. Parses regulation structure. Extracts part/section/subsection hierarchy for `section_path`. No scraping needed — structured API.

**`SourceAdapter[JusticeLaws]`** — Scrapes Justice Laws Canada. Handles bilingual HTML. Extracts `title_fr` for French documents. Flags any document with confidence < 0.7 to dead-letter queue.

**`SourceAdapter[CourtListener]`** — Consumes CourtListener REST API (CC-BY licensed). Extracts case law: citation, court, jurisdiction, opinion text, cited cases. Builds initial citation pairs for `CitationGraphAgent`.

**`SourceAdapter[StateStatutes]`** — Playwright-based scraper for state statute portals. Runs per-state with individual format adapters. Only Phase 1 states: US-CA, US-NY, US-TX, US-FL, US-WA. Fails loudly on format change detection.

*All SourceAdapter systematic functions:* source connection → document fetch → license validation (reject if not cleared) → raw archive to S3/GCS → document parse → field extraction → quality scoring → write to `legal_documents` → publish chunk tasks to Celery queue

**`ChunkingEngineer`** — Consumes documents from the Celery `chunk_document` queue. Implements hierarchical chunking: respects document structure (Act/Part/Division/Section/Subsection). Extracts `section_path`. Enforces 512-token max chunk size. Writes `legal_chunks` rows (without embeddings).

*Systematic functions:* document structure parser → hierarchy extractor → section_path builder → token counter → chunk size enforcer → overlap logic (50-token overlap) → quality score per chunk → write to `legal_chunks` → publish embed tasks to Celery

**`EmbeddingWorker`** — Consumes chunks from the Celery `embed_chunk` queue. Calls `mxbai-embed-large-v1` via Ollama in batches of 32. Writes embedding vector to `legal_chunks.embedding`. Updates `data_quality_score`. Handles Ollama retry on timeout.

*Systematic functions:* Ollama client setup → batch construction → embedding call with retry → vector write → quality score update → batch metrics logging → dead-letter on repeated failure

**Lane 3 Gate Criteria:** 1,000+ documents ingested from each Phase 1 source. All chunks have embeddings. Dead-letter queue < 2% of total documents. `ingest_jobs` shows complete run logs.

***

### Lane 4 — Search \& Intelligence *(staged: HybridSearch first, then ResearchTask, then CitationGraph)*

**`HybridSearchEngineer`** builds first. `ResearchTaskAgent` integrates on top. `CitationGraphAgent` integrates last. Not fully parallel — staged within the lane.

**`HybridSearchEngineer`** — Implements the combined pgvector cosine similarity + PostgreSQL full-text search (BM25-style `ts_rank`). Implements configurable weight injection. Builds the query builder class with jurisdiction/body-of-law/doc-type filters. Writes performance benchmarks (target: <200ms for top-10 on 1M chunks).

*Systematic functions:* embedding query builder → full-text query builder → hybrid score formula → filter injection → configurable weight system → result normalization → pagination → benchmark test → self-test: 50 known queries against golden set expectations

**`ResearchTaskAgent`** — Implements the `research_task` macro action. Integrates `HybridSearchEngineer`'s search builder. Implements question decomposition (3–5 sub-queries from complex question). Parallel asyncio search across sub-queries. Result deduplication. Structured research report assembly.

*Systematic functions:* question decomposer → parallel search orchestrator → result deduplicator → relevance ranker → gap detector (jurisdictions with no results) → report assembler → confidence scorer → freshness metadata injector → self-test: 10 complex questions against golden set

**`CitationGraphAgent`** — Implements citation pair extraction from case law text (regex + structured field). Writes citation pairs to `legal_citations`. Implements graph traversal (BFS up to depth 3). Builds chain-of-authority summaries. Detects overruled cases and flags in document record.

*Systematic functions:* citation extractor → citation type classifier → graph writer → BFS traversal → chain builder → overruled case detector → document flag updater → self-test: known precedent chain produces correct authority order

**Lane 4 Gate Criteria:** Precision@5 ≥ 0.80 on 20-question sample (pre-golden set). `research_task` returns structured report on 5 complex questions. Citation chains correct on 3 known precedent pairs.

***

### Lane 5 — Quality *(runs in parallel, against all prior outputs)*

**`TestWriter`** — Writes the complete test suite. Unit tests for every function (pytest + pytest-asyncio). Integration tests for every API endpoint (httpx.AsyncClient against test DB). Fixture data for all test scenarios. Golden set harness: 50 test cases loaded from `tests/golden_set.json`.

*Systematic functions:* unit test authoring (every pure function) → integration test authoring (every route) → fixture factory → golden set loader → test database setup/teardown → mock Ollama client → mock S3 client → coverage report → self-test: `pytest --cov=app --cov-report=term` must show >85% coverage

**`EvalRunner`** — Implements the automated evaluation harness. Calculates Precision@5, Recall@10, NDCG@10 after every ingest run. Implements LLM-as-judge prompt (calibrated against human annotations). Writes eval results to `eval_runs` table. Blocks deployment if any metric drops >5% from baseline.

*Systematic functions:* golden set loader → search caller → result scorer (Precision/Recall/NDCG) → LLM judge prompt execution → score aggregation → baseline comparison → pass/fail gate → eval dashboard metrics writer → self-test: deterministic test input produces deterministic score

**`LinterFixer`** — Runs `black` (formatter), `isort` (import sorter), `flake8` (linter), `mypy` (type checker) against the entire codebase. Auto-fixes `black` and `isort` violations. Reports `flake8` and `mypy` issues as structured JSON for `CriticAgent` review. Fails gate if any `mypy` errors remain.

*Systematic functions:* black run + auto-fix → isort run + auto-fix → flake8 run → mypy run → structured error report → auto-fix commit → remaining error report to CriticAgent → self-test: `mypy app/ --strict` must pass

**Lane 5 Gate Criteria:** >85% test coverage. All linter/type checks pass. Eval harness runs to completion on seed data. LLM-as-judge calibration complete.

***

### Lane 6 — Ops \& Docs *(runs in parallel with Lane 5)*

**`ObservabilityAgent`** — Instruments Prometheus metrics on every route and every Celery task. Writes Grafana dashboard JSON (4 panels: request rate, error rate, P95 latency, ingest jobs/hour). Configures structured JSON logging throughout with correlation IDs. Writes alert rules: error_rate > 0.5%, P95 > 400ms, ingest_lag > 2x expected cadence.

*Systematic functions:* Prometheus counter/histogram definition → metric decorator injection → Grafana dashboard JSON → Loki/structured log format definition → correlation ID middleware → alert rule YAML → self-test: generate 100 requests, verify metrics appear in Prometheus

**`SREAgent`** — Writes `/health` (liveness) and `/ready` (readiness) endpoints with DB + Redis + Ollama checks. Writes complete incident runbooks: INC-001 (source format change), INC-002 (latency spike), INC-003 (repealed law returned). Writes `oncall.md` with escalation paths. Writes backup/restore scripts.

*Systematic functions:* health endpoint implementation → readiness probe logic → runbook authoring (INC-001 through INC-005) → backup script (pg_dump + S3 upload) → restore script → SLA calculation formula → self-test: kill DB, verify `/ready` returns 503

**`DocWriter`** — Writes `README.md` (quick start, architecture, configuration), `ARCHITECTURE.md` (full system design with ADR log), `API.md` (all MCP tools + REST endpoints with examples), `AGENTS.md` initialization (the manifest template), and `LEGAL_DATA_SOURCES.md` (license status per source, CLO clearance record).

*Systematic functions:* README authoring → architecture decision records (5 ADRs: pgvector choice, Celery choice, mxbai model choice, RLS pattern, MCP gateway pattern) → API docs from OpenAPI spec → MCP tool docs → legal source license registry → self-test: run `README.md` quick start, verify system starts

**Lane 6 Gate Criteria:** Metrics appear in Prometheus on test traffic. Health endpoints return correct status. All runbooks reviewed by `CriticAgent` for completeness. README quick start verified working.

***

### AEU-7 — Integration Gate

`HordeMaster` orchestrates the final integration sequence:

1. All lane branches merged to `main` in dependency order (L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 → L5 → L6)
2. Full test suite run against integrated system: `pytest tests/ -v --cov=app`
3. Golden set evaluation run: `python evals/run_golden_set.py`
4. Canary deployment to 10% traffic (Cloud Run revision)
5. 48-hour observation window: error rate, latency, eval metrics all monitored
6. `CriticAgent` issues final gate: PROMOTE or ROLLBACK
7. On PROMOTE: full traffic cutover, `AGENTS.md` updated with v1.0.0 production tag

![](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## LEXCORE v3.0 — COMPLETE SYSTEM SPECIFICATION

### Positioning \& Business Model *(CEO + Strategy Lens)*

**Statement:** *"The only North American legal intelligence database your firm actually owns. Open ecosystem, full US+CA jurisdiction coverage, zero per-seat lock-in."*

**The Three-Layer Moat:**

- Data sovereignty — full self-host available, Harvey cannot offer this
- Cross-border coverage — US + Canada in one system, bilingual, Harvey is US-centric[^4]
- Open MCP ecosystem — any agent, any tool, any platform connects

| Tier | Target | Price | Query Limit |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| **Solo** | Solo practitioners | \$149/mo | 10K queries/mo |
| **Firm** | 10–200 attorney firms | \$799/mo | Unlimited |
| **Enterprise** | BigLaw, corporate legal, legal tech builders | \$3,500/mo | Unlimited + self-host |


***

### Data Licensing Clearance Map *(CLO Lens)*[^5][^6]

| Source | License | AI Training Safe | Action |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| eCFR | US Public Domain | ✅ | None |
| US Code | US Public Domain | ✅ | None |
| CourtListener | CC-BY 4.0 | ✅ | Attribution metadata |
| Common Corpus Legal | Open License | ✅ | Verify per-jurisdiction |
| Justice Laws Canada | Crown Copyright | ⚠️ | CLO clearance required |
| CanLII | ToS restricted | ⚠️ | API partnership negotiation |
| State statute sites | Varies | ⚠️ | Per-state ToS audit |
| PACER | Paywalled | ✅ via CourtListener mirror | Use CC-BY mirror only |

**Rule:** No source enters production ingest pipeline until `LEGAL_DATA_SOURCES.md` shows CLO clearance. `SourceAdapter` agents self-check this file before writing any documents.

***

### Three ICP Personas *(CPO + Strategy Lens)*

**Sarah Chen** — Senior Associate, Toronto cross-border M\&A. Needs fast US+CA research, citation chains, export-to-brief. Buys on Firm tier via legal ops manager. Success = research time from 4h to 30min.[^4][^7]

**Marcus Webb** — Solo criminal defense, Phoenix AZ. Needs affordable case law search, statute change alerts. Buys Solo tier (\$149/mo out of pocket). Success = never misses a statute change.[^8]

**Jennifer Park** — General Counsel, Vancouver Series B tech. Needs bilingual coverage, audit trail for board compliance, data sovereignty. Buys Enterprise tier. Needs self-hosted option. Success = defensible compliance log presented to board.[^4]

***

### Complete Stack *(CTO + Architect Lens)*

```
PostgreSQL 15 + pgvector (HNSW)    — primary data + vector store
Redis 7                             — query cache + Celery broker
Ollama (mxbai-embed-large-v1)      — local embeddings, zero API cost
Celery 5 + Celery Beat             — ingest scheduling + task queue
FastAPI 0.115                      — REST API + WebSocket
MCP Server SDK                     — agent gateway (all tools)
Docling                            — PDF/HTML extraction + OCR
Docker Compose (dev) + Cloud Run   — deployment
Prometheus + Grafana               — observability
S3/GCS                             — raw document archive (immutable)
GitHub Actions                     — CI/CD + canary deployment
```

**Phase 3 additions:** Qdrant (when vector volume exceeds pgvector optimal range), Kubernetes (when Cloud Run auto-scale insufficient), pgBouncer (connection pooling at scale).

***

### The Complete Database Schema

*(Full production schema — all tables, all indexes, RLS, multi-tenancy, audit trail, bilingual support, soft deletes, version history, ingest job log, monitor rules, query cache)*

See full schema in v2.0 section above — v3.0 schema is identical with two additions:

```sql
-- EVAL RUNS (for EvalRunner agent + regression gate)
CREATE TABLE eval_runs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  run_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  git_sha TEXT,
  precision_at_5 FLOAT,
  recall_at_10 FLOAT,
  ndcg_at_10 FLOAT,
  citation_accuracy FLOAT,
  total_test_cases INT,
  passed_cases INT,
  gate_passed BOOLEAN,
  notes TEXT
);

-- AGENT MANIFEST (mirrors AGENTS.md in DB for programmatic access)
CREATE TABLE agent_manifest (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  schema_version TEXT NOT NULL,
  lane_contracts JSONB,
  completed_outputs JSONB,
  open_blockers JSONB,
  last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);
```


***

### The MCP Tool Contracts

Seven tools. Every agent calls `get_capabilities` first to self-orient. `research_task` is the primary macro action. `search_legal` is the precision tool. All others support specific workflow needs.[^9][^10]


| Tool | Input | Output | Cache TTL |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| `get_capabilities` | none | full jurisdiction + doc type manifest | 1 hour |
| `search_legal` | query, filters, top_k, config | ranked chunks + confidence + freshness | 1 hour |
| `research_task` | question, jurisdictions, depth | full research report + citation chain | 30 min |
| `get_document` | doc_id or citation | full document + all chunks + citation list | 6 hours |
| `get_citations` | doc_id, direction, depth | citation graph up to depth N | 6 hours |
| `check_updates` | jurisdiction, since_date | all legislative changes since date | 15 min |
| `jurisdiction_summary` | jurisdiction_code | coverage %, doc count, recent changes | 1 hour |


***

### The Agent Directory *(all 7 LexCore application agents)*

| Agent | Macro Function | Primary Tools | Autonomy | Trigger |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| **LexIngest** | Full ingest pipeline end-to-end. Self-heals on source format changes. Human flag only on quality < 0.7. | Internal + Celery | 95% | Beat schedule per source |
| **LexSearch** | Complete legal research answers. Structured report output. | `research_task`, `search_legal`, `get_document` | 100% | On-demand |
| **LexAnalysis** | Cross-jurisdiction statute comparison, conflict identification, case law trend summaries. Stores outputs back to LexCore. | `research_task`, `get_citations`, `search_legal` | 85% | On-demand |
| **LexDraft** | Full legal document generation from templates + jurisdiction context. All sources cited. | `search_legal`, `get_document`, `get_capabilities` | 80% | On-demand |
| **LexMonitor** | Watches all tenant `monitor_rules`. Detects changes. Auto-drafts change summaries. Pushes alerts. | `check_updates`, `jurisdiction_summary` | 90% | Every 6 hours |
| **LexCite** | Citation verification, precedent chain analysis, overruled case detection. | `get_citations`, `get_document` | 100% | On-demand |
| **LexRouter** | Classifies inbound intent, selects agent, passes context. Calls `get_capabilities` on startup. | `get_capabilities` + all | 100% | Every inbound query |


***

### Evaluation Framework *(Testing Team Lens + Tenex 5-Stage)*[^11]

- **Stage 1:** 50 golden test cases authored by `TestWriter` agent, structured in `tests/golden_set.json`
- **Stage 2:** Legal SME (one attorney per persona type) annotates all 50 golden outputs — this is the only required human step in the entire build
- **Stage 3:** `EvalRunner` calibrates LLM-as-judge against annotations. Target: judge within 0.5 of human score
- **Stage 4:** Automated metrics per deploy — Precision@5 ≥ 0.85, Recall@10 ≥ 0.80, NDCG@10 ≥ 0.78, Citation accuracy = 100%
- **Stage 5:** Regression gate blocks any deployment that drops any metric >5% from baseline

***

### AEU-Based Execution Plan *(No Human Timelines)*

| AEU | Lane | Agents Active | Gate Criteria | Blocker |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| **AEU-0** | Command \& Control | 3 | `AGENTS.md` initialized, all contracts defined, CLO clearance for Phase 1 sources | CLO must clear CanLII + Justice Laws before AEU-1 starts |
| **AEU-1** | Foundation | 4 parallel | Migrations run clean, Docker starts all services, CI triggers | Schema contract must be published to `AGENTS.md` before AEU-2 |
| **AEU-2** | Backend Core | 4 parallel | All routes return correct codes, MCP tools schema-valid, auth rejects unauthorized, cache logs correctly | MCP tool contracts published before AEU-3 |
| **AEU-3** | Data Pipeline | 8 (IngestArchitect first, then 7 parallel) | 1K+ docs per Phase 1 source, <2% dead-letter rate, all chunks embedded | Embedding model must be running in Ollama before SourceAdapters start |
| **AEU-4** | Search \& Intel | 3 staged | Precision@5 ≥ 0.80 on 20-question sample, research_task returns reports, 3 citation chains verified | HybridSearch must complete before ResearchTask integrates |
| **AEU-5** | Quality | 3 parallel | >85% test coverage, all linters pass, eval harness operational | Legal SME annotations required for full eval calibration (async human step) |
| **AEU-6** | Ops \& Docs | 3 parallel | Metrics in Prometheus, health endpoints correct, README quick start works | None — runs concurrent with AEU-5 |
| **AEU-7** | Integration | 1 | All tests green, golden set Precision@5 ≥ 0.85, 48h canary stable | All prior gates must be passed |


***

### Phase Rollout Targets *(by data coverage, not time)*

- **Phase 1 Complete:** CanLII federal + ON/BC/AB + Justice Laws Canada bilingual = ~140K documents. 10 beta users. LexMonitor live. All eval gates passing.
- **Phase 2 Complete:** + eCFR + US Code + CourtListener federal + CA/NY/TX/FL/WA = ~800K documents. Cross-border US+CA capability live. 50 paying customers. Canary promotion stable.
- **Phase 3 Complete:** + All 50 US states + all 13 CA provinces/territories = ~3M+ documents. Full North American coverage. 200+ customers. Enterprise self-host tier live.

***

### Operating Costs *(CFO Lens)*

| Phase | Infrastructure | Break-even |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| Phase 1 | ~\$154/mo (Neon + Redis + Cloud Run + Celery workers) | 6 Solo + 2 Firm customers |
| Phase 2 | ~\$400/mo | 12 Solo + 4 Firm customers |
| Phase 3 | ~\$900/mo | Any 2 Enterprise customers |


***

**Your one required action before AEU-0 starts:** Legal SME attorney annotation of the 50 golden test cases (async — this can run during AEU-5 without blocking AEU-0 through AEU-4). Everything else is Horde-executed from spec to production.

**Green light this and the first code artifact is the complete AEU-0 package: `AGENTS.md` manifest template + `LEGAL_DATA_SOURCES.md` CLO clearance checklist + all Alembic migrations — ready to hand directly to your coding agents.**

[^2]: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/appsonazureblog/the-swarm-diaries-what-happens-when-you-let-ai-agents-loose-on-a-codebase/4501393

[^3]: https://aiautomationglobal.com/blog/agentic-coding-revolution-multi-agent-teams-2026

[^4]: https://www.latentbridge.com/insights/the-most-important-ai-trends-for-law-firms-in-2026-what-will-actually-change-in-legal-operations-and-client-delivery

[^5]: https://www.reuters.com/legal/legalindustry/copyright-law-2025-courts-begin-draw-lines-around-ai-training-piracy-market-harm--pracin-2026-03-16/

[^6]: https://www.osler.com/en/insights/reports/2025-legal-outlook/ai-training-on-trial-the-next-legal-frontier-in-copyright-law/

[^8]: https://abovethelaw.com/2025/12/five-new-ai-powered-business-models-for-solos-and-smalls-in-2026/

[^9]: https://developers.redhat.com/articles/2026/01/08/building-effective-ai-agents-mcp
